title: Today's AI Brief: Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI
summary: A look at the impact of agentic AI on organizational design and the coding world
imageAlt: Image from source article for Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI
imageCaption: Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI
imageSourceTitle: Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI

body.text: I'm diving into some interesting developments in the world of AI. It seems that as AI agents become more prevalent in enterprises, there's a growing disconnect between the ambition to leverage these tools and the actual execution. According to a recent article in Technology Review, 85% of organizations want to be 'agentic' with AI, but there's a gap in how to effectively integrate these systems. It's a bit like having a superpower, but not quite knowing how to use it.

The first article I'm looking at is from Technology Review, titled 'Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI'. The piece highlights the challenges organizations face in adopting enterprise-level AI agents. It's clear that as AI becomes more autonomous, companies need to rethink their structures to get the most out of these tools. This might involve new roles, new processes, and a new way of thinking about how work gets done.

On a related note, TechCrunch reports that coders are now refusing to work without AI tools. Researchers have found that developers are heavily reliant on AI coding tools, but the question remains whether this reliance is producing better code. I mean, think about it - if AI is just making us faster, but not necessarily better, that's a bit of a problem. It's like having a sports car that can only go fast in a straight line, but not around corners.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
